{"query":"Netanyahu Trump tactical disagreements core issues communicate every couple of days interview 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1882,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.axios.com/2026/07/04/trump-israel-netanyahu-white-house-meeting-iran","title":"Trump says Netanyahu "knows who the boss is" ahead of White House visit","content":"President Trump told Axios Saturday that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu asked him for a meeting at the White House, and that it could take place as early as next week after Trump returns from the NATO summit.
"During their conversation, the Prime Minister said that the United States is a guarantor of global freedom, and that Israel greatly values the close relationship between the two nations.
Trump lashed out at Netanyahu over Israel's escalation in Lebanon in a phone call last month, calling the prime minister "crazy" and accusing him of ingratitude."},{"url":"https://www.i24news.tv/en/news/israel/diplomacy-defense/artc-we-can-disagree-in-the-morning-go-back-to-friends-in-the-afternoon-netanyahu-responds-to-tense-trump-call","title":"'We Can Disagree In The Morning, Go Back To Friends In The Afternoon:' Netanyahu Responds To Tense Trump Call - i24NEWS","content":"Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said in an interview with CNBC on Wednesday that despite reports of a tense and profanity-laced phone call with US President Trump, the two leaders continue to maintain close coordination and mutual respect.
Addressing claims that Trump told him during a private conversation, “You’re f****** crazy,” Netanyahu declined to go into details but confirmed that disagreements occasionally arise in their discussions.
“We can disagree in the morning and in the afternoon go back to being friends,” he said, adding that the two speak “every two days.”
Regarding reported Israeli strikes in the region and the fragile security environment, Netanyahu said Israel continues to respond to rocket fire and threats against its civilians, framing operations as “surgical strikes” against militant leadership rather than broader escalation."},{"url":"https://www.chathamhouse.org/2026/07/netanyahu-caught-between-trump-and-hard-place","title":"Netanyahu is caught between Trump and a hard place | Chatham House – International Affairs Think Tank","content":"Image — US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu (L) arrive for a press conference in the State Dining Room of the White House in Washington, DC on 29 September 2025.
Trump has confirmed reports he called Netanyahu ‘crazy’ and used an expletive during a tense phone call.
A new book claims there was a similarly angry phone call just days before the public announcement of the ceasefire deal to end the war in Gaza.
He reportedly used to stall to buy time during the Biden administration, postponing key decisions such as a normalization agreement with Saudi Arabia, and fateful decisions on Gaza and the hostages until Trump returned to the White House.
Polls in Israel indicate that Netanyahu currently cannot secure a coalition."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/6/netanyahu-says-his-ties-with-trump-are-fine-takes-aim-at-turkiye","title":"Netanyahu says his ties with Trump are ‘fine’, takes aim at Turkiye | Benjamin Netanyahu News | Al Jazeera","content":"Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says his ties with US President Donald Trump are “fine”, dismissing reports of rifts between the two leaders over the ceasefire with Iran and Israel’s attacks in Lebanon.
In an interview with Fox News on Monday, Netanyahu heaped praise on the United States and Trump.
Asked about his agenda during the visit, Netanyahu took aim at Turkiye, saying that he will lobby against the transfer of F-35 jets to Ankara.
Turkiye, a NATO ally of the US, has been an outspoken critic of Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza.
Turkiye’s President Recep Tayyip Erdogan warned on Saturday against Israel’s efforts to undermine the US-Iran agreement."}]}
